behavior_summary: Implements the fire-spell and related visual/area-effect sequences: play SFX, create temporary visual items, advance their frames, spawn short-lived helper processes for timing, and destroy temporary items when complete.

Provides multiple casting/resolution entry points and variants (player-facing and non-player flows) that synchronize effects with spawn/suspend patterns, call shared resolution methods (METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7), and optionally center the camera or put the Avatar into stasis during visible sequences.

Contains iterators and placement routines that compute offsets from a source item/NPC, test legal placement, push/pop created items into the world, and adjust placements based on distance and legality (ensuring visual effects appear in valid positions).

Includes a trial/test orchestrator (U8P_FIRESPEL::func233D) that enumerates nearby targets, applies randomized damage via Item::receiveHit, emits bark lines for success/failure, and updates trial state (inferred trial flags/states).
notable_control_flow: Heavy use of spawn + suspend: many functions spawn FREE::func1C19 or other helper processes to implement timed delays and then suspend until the helper completes — spawn/suspend is the primary timing mechanism.
Player-visible branches (this == 0x01) typically setAvatarInStasis(0x01) at start and clear it on exit to prevent player input during the scripted animation sequence.
Casting flows capture menu/choice results via suspend into local variables (local1 / param1) and then branch on those values to decide player vs non-player behavior or target selection.

Multi-step placement/update loops: functions like U8P_FIRESPEL::func26EF and U8P_FIRESPEL::func00A9 sample item coords, spawn/await item-specific METHOD processes, re-check distance/map changes, and either push existing items or recreate them as needed.

Several small predicate helpers (U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AC7, U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AF5, U8P_FIRESPEL::func2B3E) gate branches that choose explosion/destroy paths or recursive retries based on item type/frame.
open_questions: Precise semantics of METHOD::func15E7 and METHOD::func091C: they appear to apply staged resolution/impact or advance effect state, but their exact game-state effects (damage, summon, status) are not visible here (confidence: medium).

Exact meanings and canonical scope of named flags referenced in analyses (MalchirTest, malchirMad, avatarEndure, avatarArmor): section analysis indicates they are read/written, but aggregate metadata lists no global flags — are these global game flags or local/script-only flags? (confidence: low).

Numeric tuning: the damage bounds, frame indices used for branching, and the constants added to coordinates (0x40, 0x60, 0x18, etc.) are concrete in code but their gameplay mapping (tile/pixel scale, damage scaling) needs verification against engine docs.

Some small predicate helpers (U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AC7/U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AF5/U8P_FIRESPEL::func2B3E) have simple checks on Item::getType or Item::getFrame; the intended target categories (e.g., which types correspond to destructible objects vs. immune ones) remain uncertain without the type map.

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func0080
main_function.purpose: Short-lived lifetime/timer cleanup for a temporary effect item: spawn a tiny free/timer helper (FREE::func1C19), suspend until it completes, then destroy the item (Item::destroy(this)). Acts as an item-self cleanup for ephemeral visuals.
entry_conditions: Spawned when a temporary effect/item is created and a self-contained cleanup process is attached.
Runs autonomously (no explicit player menu/dialog) and waits on a spawned helper to elapse before cleanup.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func0080
function_map.role: lifetime/cleanup process for temporary effect items

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func00A9
function_map.role: multi-step placement/animation: create temporary items around a source, spawn per-item METHOD animators, adjust placement via legal_move and FREE::maxDelta3D

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func059A
function_map.role: player/NPC casting orchestrator variant: capture menu choice, set avatar stasis, play SFX, call resolution methods, create visuals as needed

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func0AF5
function_map.role: alternate casting/orchestration flow similar to U8P_FIRESPEL::func059A; branches on target types and spawns create/destroy paths

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func0E47
function_map.role: parameterized casting flow (param1,param2,param3) with player and non-player branches; spawns placement helpers and coordinates SFX/METHOD calls

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::playFireSpellSFX
function_map.role: play the concrete fire-spell audio (wrapper for playSFX with fixed IDs)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func12DF
function_map.role: menu/options builder and timed placement iterator: computes offsets from caster mana, loops candidate positions with canExistAt checks

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func177A
function_map.role: short resolution sequence: create temp item, call U8P_FIRESPEL::func26EF for frame updates, manage avatar stasis and SFX

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func195D
function_map.role: casting sequence that queries caster int (Npc::getInt), spawns FIRESPEL::func21A2, and conditionally plays SFX, then finalizes with METHOD::func15E7

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func1AFE
function_map.role: another casting/resolution orchestrator: compute direction, spawn U8P_FIRESPEL::func1FE4, spawn U8P_FIRESPEL::func233D as background, manage avatar stasis

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func1CEB
function_map.role: variant of resolution sequence (mana-scaled) that creates/positions temp items and calls U8P_FIRESPEL::func26EF

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func1F65
function_map.role: create short-lived visual item (type 0x01E0h), play SFX, optionally advance its frame then destroy it (timed life)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func1FE4
function_map.role: place/create temporary item around 'this' based on param2 selector, spawn METHOD::func013E on it, wait, and return the created item reference

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func21A2
function_map.role: attempt to create a persistent visual/effect item, run a short delay, then finalize the item with SFX and spawn follow-up (U8P_FIRESPEL::func229E)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func229E
function_map.role: countdown and finalization/cleanup helper for created effect items: loop with this_hi countdown or spawn final effect then destroy self

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func233D
function_map.role: trial/test orchestrator: toggle status flag, touch item to enumerate neighbors, add menu options, apply Item::receiveHit and emit trial barks; updates trial state (inferred)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func26EF
function_map.role: animate/update loop for temporary items: advance frames, detect movement/map changes, recreate or push/pop items until param2 countdown expires

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func2A1B
function_map.role: small countdown/pacer: preserve map across suspend, decrement param3, when zero call METHOD::func08B9 and potentially destroy target

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AC7
function_map.role: type-membership predicate: returns 1 for specific item types (0x01C5,0x01C6,0x01C4)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AF5
function_map.role: frame-membership predicate: returns 1 when item frame ∈ {0x00,0x04,0x08,0x0C}

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESPEL::func2B3E
function_map.role: wrapper predicate inverting U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AF5 result (returns temp flag based on U8P_FIRESPEL::func2AF5 outcome)

internal_calls.purpose: spawn a short free/timer helper and suspend until it completes (timing for lifetime)
spawn visual helper at computed offsets to coordinate placement and animation timing
spawn per-item animator/resolver to run item-specific animation logic after creation/pop
play central fire-spell audio during player cast branches
in player branch spawn auxiliary placement/animation routine to build player-visible visuals
spawn the created item's METHOD::func013E to animate/resolve it and suspend until completion
apply damage/hit to nearby targets using a random range computed per-branch
use repeated spawn+suspend to pace frame advancement or placement updates

external_calls.purpose: destroy the temporary/self item after lifetime expired
play concrete SFX IDs for the fire spell (audio feedback)
freeze the Avatar during casting sequences in player-facing branches
perform an intermediate resolution/pre-impact action when local1 != 1 (>0) before main resolution
stage resolution/impact ticks or advance effect state at multiple points of the casting flow
create and position temporary visual items to realize the spell's visuals
create a short-lived visual item, optionally advance its frame, then destroy it
compute randomized damage amounts for Item::receiveHit
start a repeating pentagram SFX/visual loop on the created effect item
run a per-item animation/preparation before final resolution/destroy; test process_result() to decide follow-up

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the fire-spell and related visual/area-effect sequences: play SFX, create temporary visual items, advance their frames, spawn short-lived helper processes for timing, and destroy temporary items when complete.
